nation: Conseil des Montagnais de Unamen Shipu
economic_notes: Band owns interests in: Corporation de développement économique de Unamen Shipu (100%), Voyages Inter Nations et Associés inc. (33.33%), Pêcheries Unamen Shipu s.e.c. (51%), Pourvoirie Étamamiou inc. (100%), Société Mukutan inc. (100%), Les Pêcheries Amik Unamen (50%), InnuVoyages inc. (100%), Motel Madame Ruby inc. (100%), Société en commandite Apuiat (12.48%), 9321-1654 Québec inc. (12.48%). All accounted for under modified equity method.
notes: Qualified audit opinion (opinion avec réserves) from MNP for multiple reasons: (1) non-compliance with SP 3280 asset retirement obligations, (2) inability to observe year-end inventory count, (3) 6 subsidiary companies accounted for under modified equity method with no audited financial statements available, (4) prior year Centre de santé de Unamen Shipu balances unaudited since April 2020. Prior year figures restated (note 4) including corrections to partnership investments (+$6,209,863), inventory (+$870,000), accounts receivable adjustments for health centre and SSNA programs. Revenue and expense figures are split between operating fund and capital fund; total revenue calculated as 69,946,297 + 8,933,662 = 78,879,959 and total expenses as 70,304,426 + 3,039,979 = 73,344,405. ISC fixed contributions (Fonds inutilisés) of $14,898,001. Remuneration schedules are present at pages 250 and 252 but detailed amounts not extracted from provided text. Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments on long-term debt ($772,491) plus lease obligation repayments ($29,909) = $802,400. Province is Quebec (QC) — Innu community on the Lower North Shore.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
